intent: A large log: 420 AVC records that collapse to 15 distinct (sdomain, ttype, tclass) triples across 12 confined domains. Each triple is repeated ~28 times (the flood). Tests grouping correctness, dedup (420 records MUST become 15 allows, not 420), output that does not flood, and performance at scale. Also a fidelity guard: every type used is a real type in the el10 targeted policy (a synthetic type that does not resolve would be silently dropped by audit2allow - see RUN-note below).
_kinds_note: All 15 triples TeAllowable. floor_label none for all (same role, level s0). Some triples (httpd dir:search, var_log:append, crond user_home getattr, logrotate var_log:dir read) report 'allowed in current policy' in audit2allow comments - still valid input records.

oracle.audit2allow_N: 15 allow lines (one per distinct triple), grouped into per-domain #===== sections; audit2allow deduplicates the 420 records to 15 rules. Our expected.te allow set is IDENTICAL to -N (perm-order normalized).
oracle.audit2allow_R: Each raw allow replaced by a broad refpolicy interface macro per domain (files_read_default_files, files_search_default, files_append_log_files, etc.). Far broader than the exact per-triple perm sets.
oracle.narrower_than_R_by: -R substitutes a broad interface for nearly every triple (default_t/var_t/var_log_t all have read/write/append _perms macros that pad the perm set and may grant on whole type families). expected.te keeps 15 exact raw allows, each the literal denied perm union for its triple.

triage_expectation.suspected_trap: Three traps. (1) FLOOD: a naive impl that emits one allow per RECORD would print 420 identical-ish lines instead of 15 - triage MUST dedup by triple. (2) GROUPING: 15 triples across 12 domains with httpd_t holding 3 triples; the report must group all of a domain's triples together with deterministic ordering. (3) FIDELITY (corpus-build lesson): two of the original synthetic types (ntpd_t, rsyslogd_t) do NOT exist in the el10 targeted policy and were SILENTLY DROPPED by audit2allow - replaced with the real domains gpsd_t and syslogd_t. A triage impl replaying against a policy must surface (not silently drop) a record whose context type is undefined in the supplied policy, else denials vanish without warning.
